page: Amazon Web Services r8i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with a dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. This CPU configuration delivers 24 physical cores and 48 virtual cores (vCPUs) operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor includes an L3 cache of 480 MB, alongside 48 KB L1d and 2 MB L2 caches. It supports advanced instruction sets including Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r8i.12xlarge demonstrates strong memory and processing capabilities. Memory bandwidth testing shows strong performance, reaching 3860.19 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 192.27 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, though large-block write bandwidth is average at 77.54 GB/sec. Computational performance is highlighted by top-tier results in Geekbench single-core scoring, image processing, and text processing. Multi-threaded decompression via bzip3 is strong at 333.56 GB/sec. For database workloads, the instance achieves a strong Passmark database operations score and processes 87.47 million Redis SET operations per second. It also exhibits strong capabilities in low-latency tasks, processing 135M parameter LLM text generation at a top-tier rate of 346.76 tokens per second, and maintaining strong prompt processing speeds across 7B and 70B models.

The r8i.12xlarge offers high resource density for memory-bound applications, utilizing DDR5 memory and a high L3 cache allocation to optimize data-retrieval speeds. However, the lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency tradeoffs for disk-heavy operations. The instance is highly suited for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, Redis caching layers, large-scale software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks that benefit from its AMX instruction support and high memory-to-vCPU ratio.
description: Amazon Web Services r8i.12xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring a dedicated 24-core Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 vCPUs running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, a 480 MB L3 cache, and a baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps, though it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in single-core processing, image manipulation, and text processing, alongside strong memory read bandwidth of up to 3860.19 GB/sec. The instance also demonstrates strong database capabilities, executing 87.47 million Redis SET operations per second, and strong LLM inference speeds. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, software compilation, and text processing pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering top-tier single-core and strong database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r8i.12xlarge is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 3.9 GHz Intel Xeon CPU.
tagline: A memory-optimized server featuring high-speed DDR5 RAM, dedicated Intel Xeon processing, and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 48 vCPUs and 3.9 GHz clock speed
384.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory with 480 MB L3 cache
Top-tier single-core, image processing, and text processing benchmark results
Strong database performance with 87.47 million Redis SET operations per second
Baseline network bandwidth of 22 Gbps with no local storage or GPU
Best-fit for in-memory databases, caching layers, and software compilation
